flags: Concentration: SPY is 30% of the book

moves.note: 3x semi ETF; worst performer, cuts tail risk
Best R/R 9.46, only if entry triggers
Semi exposure via quality, not leverage
Diversifies away from pure chip concentration

plan: RISK FIRST: Exit SOXL and SPAL immediately — together they represent 8% of the book, are down hard (-14.5% and -13.7%), and both use leverage; removing them lowers your worst-case 2-sigma month from -17.91% and cuts true leverage closer to 1.0x.
Trim SMH from 13% to 7% — semiconductors are already in SPY, QQQ, and the new TSM/ARM ideas, so 13% dedicated semi exposure is redundant concentration that amplifies chip-cycle drawdowns.

Use the freed capital (~14% of book) to fund new entries: Buy PANW up to 5% at the scanner entry zone (351.34–351.69, hard stop 350.62) — it has the highest conviction score (9/12) and a clean R/R of 6.01.
Buy TSM up to 4% — this replaces levered SMH with a direct, unlevered best-in-class semiconductor name that diversifies away from US-only ETF overlap.

Set conditional Watch orders for ARM (4%) and CRM (3%) only if price trades into the scanner entry ranges — ARM's R/R of 9.46 is exceptional but do NOT chase; CRM adds software diversification away from chips.

After all moves: total adds (~12%) are funded by total trims/sells (~14%), keeping a small cash buffer, true leverage stays near or below 1.0x, and your worst-case single-month loss shrinks meaningfully before you add any new risk.
read: A consistent 2.5%/month (~34% annualized) is at the aggressive end of realistic for a retail margin book — it is achievable in strong trending months but this portfolio's current -17.91% two-sigma downside means one bad month can erase 7+ months of gains, which mathematically breaks the consistency needed to compound toward that target.
